summary: Hardcoded Telegram bot token enables cross-installer remote command execution
details: stella-cli/telegram-bot.mjs embeds a hardcoded Telegram bot token (BOT_TOKEN = "8923551485:...") shipped in every published tarball, and uses it to poll https://api.telegram.org/bot<token> for messages. When the installer activates the /tg feature, handleMessage passes text received via Telegram straight into execSync(`node stella-cli/index.mjs -p "<text>"`), and the -p mode in index.mjs forces askPermission to auto-return true, so all built-in tools (bash, write_file, edit_file) run without prompting. Access is gated only by a locally generated 4-digit admin code. Because the bot token is a shared secret baked into the public npm artifact, any party who reads the tarball can talk to the bot as its owner, observe messages from Telegram users controlling their own Stella instances, capture the 4-digit admin codes typed by those users, and then issue commands that Stella executes as arbitrary shell on the installer's host. telegram-bot.mjs also collects os.hostname() and os.userInfo().username into the Telegram message stream. The package's main entry (stella-cli/index.mjs) is a self-executing interactive CLI that runs main() at top level, so require('stella-coder') has side effects (terminal clear, API-key prompts, REPL) beyond library loading.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
